title: Reading Pulse Columns
prompt:
Create a production-ready Reading Pulse Columns effect using React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. Design a compact scrollable reading surface containing three short column cards. As the user scrolls the native container, a single lime pulse moves across a header ruler and the current column gains a clear textual Current label. This must communicate reading position without hijacking page scrolling or requiring a mouse wheel animation. Use #F4F5F6 around the surface, white column cards, #ECECEE borders, 22px outer corners, soft neutral shadows, #111111 headings, and #4A4A4F support copy. Lime is the only bright accent; do not put gradients on cards.

Use a focusable native overflow container with an accessible label, semantic article sections, and a polite live region for the current column. The rail is decorative; the text label conveys progress. Support keyboard scrolling naturally and do not capture unrelated key presses. Use a spring at stiffness 290 and damping 26 for the pulse, and 180ms opacity/transform transitions for cards entering view. Do not use timers, random values, browser storage, remote media, or network calls. Keep all measurements deterministic and local.

At 320px, retain a usable 220px scroll window with readable cards; at larger widths, allow the same rail and columns to breathe. Maintain AA contrast, clear 3px dark focus styles, and logical reading order. Prefer transform and opacity. With prefers-reduced-motion, leave cards static and update only the current text or use a 120ms fade. Return complete TypeScript React client code and a runnable pure CSS approximation with suggested HTML.
